description_long: This is a rectangular wooden table with a plank-style top composed of several long, parallel boards. The surface shows a natural wood grain with some visible wear and slight discoloration, suggesting outdoor use or exposure to the elements. The table is supported by three sets of sturdy, curved legs, each set forming an arch at the base for stability. The legs appear to be made of the same wood as the tabletop, with a slightly darker finish, and are evenly spaced along the length of the table. The overall design is simple and functional, with no visible decorative elements or attached seating. This table would be well-suited for parks, picnic areas, or other communal outdoor urban spaces.
description: A rectangular wooden table with a plank-style top and three sets of arched wooden legs, showing natural wood grain and outdoor wear.
description_view_0: The table is seen from the short end, highlighting the plank-style top and one set of arched legs directly beneath.
description_view_1: A side view showing the full length of the tabletop and all three sets of arched legs, emphasizing the table's elongated form.
description_view_2: The opposite short end view, similar to view 0, with the plank divisions and a single set of legs visible.
description_view_3: Another side view mirroring view 1, displaying the table's length and the three supporting leg sets.
